content:
9 min read
Just now

--

The government approved a classifier, not a model. Then a benchmark measured the difference.
On July 1, the benchmark group BridgeMind reran its debugging suite against the freshly restored Claude Fable 5. The score fell from 86.2 to 25.9, a seventy percent collapse, and the model dropped near the bottom of a forty two model table it had led three weeks earlier.
Here is the part that should stop you: the weights are identical to June 9. Anthropic confirms it. BridgeMind confirms it. The model that got banned and the model that came back are the same model.
What changed sits outside the weights entirely. And once you see what the United States government actually reviewed, tested, and signed off on, you will read every frontier model release differently.

Nineteen Days, Zero Weight Changes
